objective: Bootstrap the next issue-backed child under issue #615 for local mutation-time pre-tool fail-closed hardening on the bounded local hook and preflight surfaces only, inheriting the planning contract from issue #619.
scope_boundary: Owns the local mutation-time pre-tool path only: .claude/settings.json, hooks/code-write-gate.sh, hooks/schema-guard.sh, scripts/overlord/check_plan_preflight.py, focused tests, and issue-local artifacts.
Inherits the bounded proof contract from closed planning lane #619.
Must reference issue #617 as the already-closed startup/helper child and issue #621 as the already-closed root backlog/commit-parity child rather than reopening their owned surfaces.

Keeps #607 as the planning-authority dependency, #612 as the degraded-fallback schema and CI enforcement lane, #614 as the downstream consumer verifier lane, and leaves broader hook-stack or CI rewrites for separate lanes.
Starts by producing the issue-local planning packet and validation evidence in this isolated worktree.
out_of_scope: Reopening issue #617 startup or helper surfacing work.
Reopening issue #621 root backlog or commit-progression parity work.
Planning-authority contract rewrites that belong to issue #607.
Execution-scope schema, fallback-log schema, or CI validator changes that belong to issue #612.
Downstream hldpro-sim consumer verifier or drift-gate work that belongs to issue #614.
Broad rewrites of hooks/backlog-check.sh, hooks/governance-check.sh, or scripts/orchestrator/delegation_gate.py.
Any claim of repo-wide write blocking closure.
research_summary: Parent issue #615, merged child #617, merged child #621, and closed planning lane #619 leave a narrow residual slice: local mutation-time pre-tool fail-closed hardening for governance-surface writes. The owned surfaces remain the same bounded local path defined in #619: .claude/settings.json, hooks/code-write-gate.sh, hooks/schema-guard.sh, scripts/overlord/check_plan_preflight.py, focused tests, and issue-local proof artifacts only. Specialist review confirmed #623 must stay on these local mutation surfaces only and must not reopen #617 or #621 or absorb #607, #612, or #614.

sprints.name: Packet Bootstrap
sprints.goal: Create the issue-local planning packet and execution-scope boundaries before local mutation-path changes.
tasks: Create the issue-local PDCAR, structured plan, execution scope, handoff, review packet, review placeholders, cross-review note, validation artifact, and closeout artifact.
Restate the narrowed local mutation-only write surfaces confirmed by specialist review.
Keep the lane in planning_only mode until the packet is validated and alternate-family review is recorded.
acceptance_criteria: The packet names the exact local mutation-only owned surfaces.
The packet keeps #617, #621, #607, #612, and #614 as explicit external boundaries.
The execution scope allows only issue-local planning artifacts during bootstrap.
The packet states that implementation is blocked until planning validation and alternate-family review complete.
The packet requires a later per-surface proof matrix and bounded-write evidence rather than generic prose-only closure.

sprints.name: Implementation Slice
sprints.goal: Implement the bounded local mutation-time pre-tool fail-closed hardening path.
tasks: Wire PreToolUse to Edit and MultiEdit in .claude/settings.json and keep Bash on schema-guard.sh.
Make hooks/code-write-gate.sh fail closed for in-scope malformed payloads, missing helper execution, unknown preflight decisions, and validator/preflight failures on the owned mutation path.
Make hooks/schema-guard.sh fail closed when the preflight helper is missing, returns malformed JSON, or returns an unknown decision for an in-scope Bash write target.

Switch scripts/overlord/check_plan_preflight.py to canonical docs/plans structured-plan evidence and expand Bash write-target detection to the named mutation verbs from #615 while preserving read-only pipelines.
Return focused tests, exact blocked and allowed transcripts, required governance doc co-staging, Stage 6 closeout evidence, and local-ci-gate proof.
acceptance_criteria: Implementation stays inside .claude/settings.json, OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md, hooks/code-write-gate.sh, hooks/schema-guard.sh, scripts/overlord/check_plan_preflight.py, focused tests, required governance doc co-staging, and issue-local artifacts only.
Edit, MultiEdit, and named Bash mutation verbs on governed surfaces are routed through hard fail-closed local enforcement with canonical docs/plans structured-plan evidence.
Read-only or out-of-scope commands remain allowed with stable behavior.
The implementation returns a per-surface proof matrix with exact blocked and allowed transcripts, bounded-write evidence, and issue-local validation / closeout artifacts.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify the narrowest valid owned slice under issue #615 after issue #621 merged, while inheriting issue #619's bounded contract.
specialist_reviews.summary: The narrowest valid issue #623 slice is mutation-time pre-tool fail-closed hardening on .claude/settings.json, hooks/code-write-gate.sh, hooks/schema-guard.sh, scripts/overlord/check_plan_preflight.py, focused tests, and issue-local artifacts only.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify the planning-only bootstrap packet shape, issue/branch alignment, and upfront AC and proof obligations.
specialist_reviews.summary: Mirror issue #619's planning-only packet shape, but repeat issue #623's exact owned and forbidden surfaces across the PDCAR, structured plan, and handoff and preserve #617, #621, #607, #612, and #614 as explicit boundaries.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify what would make the lane invalid and what proof obligations the ACs must carry if the lane is truly bounded to local mutation-time pre-tool hardening.
specialist_reviews.summary: The lane becomes invalid if it reclaims #617 startup work, #621 root parity work, absorbs #607 or #612, broadens into general hook-stack cleanup, or claims repo-wide closure. The ACs must require concrete blocked and allowed transcripts with exact local-path proof.

alternate_model_review.summary: Claude alternate-family review approved the planning-only packet with no blocking findings. Non-blocking notes were limited to future implementation-scope hygiene: enumerate doc co-staging paths explicitly when the implementation execution scope is created, and keep the handoff-state wording honest during promotion.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #623 is authorized for the bounded local mutation-time pre-tool implementation slice on .claude/settings.json, OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md, hooks/code-write-gate.sh, hooks/schema-guard.sh, scripts/overlord/check_plan_preflight.py, focused tests, required governance doc co-staging, and issue-local artifacts only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement the bounded mutation-time pre-tool fail-closed slice under the implementation execution scope and return exact blocked and allowed proof transcripts.
blocked_on: Issue #617 remains the already-closed startup/helper child and must not be reopened here.
Issue #621 remains the already-closed root backlog/commit-parity child and must not be reopened here.
Issue #607 remains the planning-authority dependency for the parent governance path.
Issue #612 remains the degraded-fallback schema and CI enforcement lane.
Issue #614 remains the downstream hldpro-sim verifier or drift-gate lane.
Implementation is blocked outside the accepted implementation execution scope and handoff.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: AC1: Issue #623 remains bounded to .claude/settings.json, OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md, hooks/code-write-gate.sh, hooks/schema-guard.sh, scripts/overlord/check_plan_preflight.py, focused tests, docs/PROGRESS.md, docs/FEATURE_REGISTRY.md, docs/FAIL_FAST_LOG.md, and issue-local artifacts only, and implementation proof must include bounded-write evidence showing the actual diff stayed inside those owned surfaces and approved co-staging.

AC2: Edit, MultiEdit, and named Bash mutation verbs on governed surfaces are hard-blocked without valid canonical docs/plans structured-plan evidence, while one compliant governance-surface mutation and one read-only or out-of-scope case remain allowed.

AC3: Helper-missing, malformed payload, parse-failure, unknown-decision, and validator/preflight failure conditions on the owned local mutation path fail closed rather than warning or silently allowing.
AC4: The implementation explicitly preserves #617, #621, #607, #612, and #614 as external boundaries rather than absorbing their owned surfaces or contracts.

AC5: Implementation proof for issue #623 returns a per-surface proof matrix with exact blocked and allowed local-path transcripts, named Bash mutation verbs, focused tests, required governance doc co-staging, Stage 6 closeout evidence, local-ci-gate proof, and no broader #615 closure claim.

material_deviation_rules: Any edit to hooks/pre-session-context.sh or scripts/overlord/check_execution_environment.py from issue #623 is a material deviation because those surfaces were already owned by issue #617.
Any edit to hooks/backlog-check.sh or hooks/governance-check.sh from issue #623 is a material deviation because those surfaces were already owned by issue #621.
Any edit to assert_execution_scope.py, fallback-log schema surfaces, or CI workflow enforcement from issue #623 is a material deviation because those belong to issue #612.
Any broad rewrite of scripts/orchestrator/delegation_gate.py or generalized hook-stack cleanup from issue #623 is a material deviation unless the issue is rerouted.
Any implementation of local mutation-path behavior before the issue-local planning packet is validated and alternate-family reviewed is a material deviation.
Any claim that issue #623 closes repo-wide write blocking or fresh-session mis-routing is a material deviation.
